summary: @dervix/ws is a typosquatted `ws` fork containing an import-time remote-code-execution dropper
details: `@dervix/ws` typosquats the popular `ws` WebSocket package. `lib/websocket.js` contains the legitimate ws source followed by two heavily-obfuscated IIFEs (obfuscator.io-style RC4 string array, per-call index rotation, control-flow flattening, and a self-defense debugger-trap regex over `toString()`). `index.js` requires `./lib/websocket`, so the obfuscated code executes on every `require()`/`import` of the package. The payload sets a sentinel environment variable and detached-respawns `process.execPath` with the original argv (`child_process.spawn(..., {detached:true, stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true})`), then constructs a URL at runtime and issues `http.request`/`https.request` (with 3xx redirect handling, retries, and timeouts) to stream an opaque blob into `os.tmpdir()/<name>-<pid>/<file>`. The blob is decrypted with `crypto.createDecipheriv('aes-256-gcm', key, iv)` + `setAuthTag`, chmodded 0o755, and executed via `spawn(process.execPath, [decryptedPath], {stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true, detached:true})`. Net effect: importing the package fetches and runs an attacker-controlled encrypted Node payload on the installer's machine, with anti-analysis wrapping that keeps the payload opaque to static scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
